description: Phase 1: Expanded benchmarks 40->120 (30/topic). Added metadata fields. Discovered 3 hint leak vulnerabilities.

Iteration 6: Independent answer verifier + semantic quality checks. Mutation kill rate 8.3% -> 41.7%. Fixed average generator IEEE 754 float division. Added answer_verifier.py with 4 topic verifiers + mathematical invariants. Integrated into benchmark runner, mutation testing, and full cycle.

Iteration 7: Wording consistency validator + average template fix. Fixed template_index=0 hardcoded san-ci (3 exams) -> uses {n} for actual count. New wording_validator.py checks problem text matches parameters. Removed stale quality check. Integrated into benchmark runner and mutation testing.

Iteration 8: Fraction mutation kill rate 10%% -> 30%%. Added 2 output-based quality checks to mutator: (1) single_denominator_one_not_fraction catches mutations that simplify a fraction to a whole number (den=1 after generator simplification), (2) lcd_too_complex_for_grade catches mutations producing LCD > 24 (too complex for grade 5). Overall mutation kill rate 40.8%% -> 47.5%%. +8 kills, all from fraction topic.

Iteration 9: Equivalent mutation detection + unit_conversion quality hardening. (1) Added equivalent mutation detector: flags mutations that produce the exact same answer as the original benchmark case (e.g. swap_ab for commutative addition, template_shift that only changes wording). Caught 24 equivalent mutations. (2) Added value_one_reverse_trivial check for unit_conversion (1 small-unit -> trivially small decimal). (3) Added conversion_answer_too_large safety net (>= 100000). Overall mutation kill rate 47.5%% -> 64.2%%. +20 kills. Gold candidates 1 -> 9.
